affected_groups: Smaller, less-resourced maintainers facing new compliance burdens
Policymakers negotiating coalition and concordat commitments
content.decision_question: Equity (uniform, SES-aware admission standards to widen opportunity) versus subsidiarity/institutional autonomy (the right of church and foundation maintainers to set their own admission criteria consistent with their constitutionally and contractually protected identity)
content.evidence_boundary: Contested and largely unknown: there is no settled evidence on whether a specific SES-proxy or quota design can be made capacity-additive and voluntary while still achieving meaningful desegregation effects; the legal question of what precisely the constitutional/concordat autonomy protections permit or forbid in this domain has not been tested in this exact form, and administrative capacity gaps across maintainer types are asserted but not quantified
content.tension: Uniform admission quotas and thresholds risk overriding church and foundation maintainer autonomy over admissions, protected by subsidiarity principles and constitutional/coalition commitments.
content.title: Decision tension A3: S1
value_poles: Equity (uniform, SES-aware admission standards to widen opportunity)
subsidiarity/institutional autonomy (the right of church and foundation maintainers to set their own admission criteria consistent with their constitutionally and contractually protected identity)
